statement.en: “The Second Reformation” in Anhalt. On the promotion of Calvinism in the German States of the late 16th — early 17th centuries
statement.summary: The article examines the Calvinist or «second» Reformation in the small German principality of Anhalt in the late 16th – early 17th centuries. Using Anhalt as an example, it is considered how the choice of religion in the era of confessional schism and the search for the «true faith» influenced the small states of the Holy Roman Empire. The religious views of German rulers were inextricably linked with their political strategy, which played a leading role in the era that historians have called «confessional». This implies the comprehensive influence of religious attitudes on the life of society. Even contemporaries emphasized the inextricable connection between religious and political issues in this period. Protestant religious practices allowed the Anhalt princes to strengthen their power

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a historical scholarship article (preprint, dated 2026-04-25) examining documented but interpretively contested period. The claims are evidence-based but subject to historiographical debate and revision. Not yet peer-validated in this version.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 信仰の選択が古い伝統を割ることで、君主の権力が国家を新しく形作る。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 円環から矩形へ：信仰が統治構造を根底から再編する時代の転換を映す。
